title: [proof-gap] Gauge-splitting G_Gamma=G^circ.R: component-occupancy (theta_0 in N_Gamma) is asserted, not proved

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Type:** imprecise/unproved step (component-occupancy of the gauge group; refines prior Gaps 1,2,4).

**Location:** `eq:gaugesplit` in §`sec:loop` (and the verbatim multiloop analogue).

**The claim.** An arbitrary equivariant gauge Psi is factored Psi = theta_0 . Psi^circ with theta_0 a CONSTANT representative of the connected component of G containing Psi(G_m), and it is asserted that one may take theta_0 in N_Gamma(g) and Psi^circ in G^circ (= inner gauge loops satisfying the ORIGINAL sigma-equivariance Psi^circ(zeta x)=sigma Psi^circ(x) sigma^{-1}).

**Why this needs justification.** Substituting Psi=theta_0 Psi^circ into the equivariance Psi(zeta x)=sigma Psi(x) sigma^{-1} gives
   Psi^circ(zeta x) = (theta_0^{-1} sigma theta_0 sigma^{-1}) . sigma Psi^circ(x) sigma^{-1}.
The prefactor theta_0^{-1} sigma theta_0 sigma^{-1} lies in Inn(g)=G^circ (since theta_0^{-1} sigma theta_0 ~ sigma), but it equals the IDENTITY — which is what is needed for Psi^circ to satisfy the same sigma-equivariance and hence lie in G^circ — only if conjugation by theta_0 fixes the OUTER class of sigma, i.e. theta_0 stabilizes the outer class of <sigma>. So:
(i) not every constant component representative theta_0 is admissible;
(ii) the claim that the *occupied* components are exactly those with an admissible constant theta_0 in N_Gamma(g) is asserted, not proved.

**Required fix.** Prove the component-occupancy lemma: the well-defined map (G_m connected) G_Gamma(G_m,g) -> Out(g) has image EQUAL to the stabilizer in Out(g) of the outer class of <sigma>. Then state the splitting G_Gamma = G^circ . R with R a transversal of (the realizable part of) that stabilizer, and identify R with the diagram symmetries of Kac's classification. Without this, the final "Out(L) = stabilizer of bar-sigma in Out(g)" conclusion is not rigorously established.
